title: Samanea pistaciifolia
taxonomy: Samanea pistaciifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. It belongs to the subfamily Caesalpinioideae and the genus Samanea. Samanea pistaciifolia is native to South America, specifically in Brazil and Paraguay. The plant is known for its large, flat leaves and its ability to fix nitrogen from the air, making it a valuable species for reforestation efforts. Samanea pistaciifolia is often used as an ornamental plant in tropical regions due to its attractive foliage and ability to tolerate a wide range of soil conditions.
